_README: Per-entity ELT connector config — SCAFFOLD / TEMPLATE. Copy per entity, fill in, keep OUT of version control if you add real ids. CREDENTIALS ARE NEVER STORED HERE: only the NAMES of environment variables (and a token-store path / private-key path) that hold them at runtime. Extraction facts + failure modes: ../knowledge/finance-elt-connector-facts.md. Staging transform: ../skills/finance-elt-staging/SKILL.md. NetSuite: this template ships TWO alternate auth blocks below — `auth` (generic OAuth2 authorization-code / TBA-era shape) and `auth_netsuite_m2m_variant` (the OAuth2 M2M cert-signed-JWT shape, the RECOMMENDED PRIMARY for any NEW NetSuite integration). Keep only the one matching your chosen flow; delete the other. Runbook: ../skills/netsuite-close/SKILL.md.

source_ids._note: Fill in ONLY the id for the chosen source_system; leave the rest null.

auth._rotating_refresh_token_note: TRUE for QBO and Xero — their refresh token ROTATES on every refresh. The new token MUST be persisted atomically (temp file + rename) BEFORE the new access token is used, under a per-entity file lock; alert on invalid_grant and follow the re-auth runbook. See ../knowledge/finance-elt-connector-facts.md.

auth_netsuite_m2m_variant._note: OPTIONAL ALTERNATE to the `auth` block above. Use THIS block instead when source_system="netsuite" and you are wiring OAuth2 M2M (client-credentials + a certificate-signed JWT client assertion) — the RECOMMENDED PRIMARY path for any NEW NetSuite integration. The plain `auth` block above (oauth2_authorization_code / TBA-era shape) is a TIME-BOXED FALLBACK ONLY: NetSuite TBA (OAuth 1.0a) cannot create new integrations from release 2027.1, with existing TBA support running to roughly 2028.1 [unverified — settling gate]. Delete whichever of the two auth blocks you are not using. Runbook: ../skills/netsuite-close/SKILL.md.
auth_netsuite_m2m_variant._no_refresh_token_note: M2M has NO refresh token at all, unlike the rotating-refresh disciplines elsewhere in this template. A fresh access token is minted each time by re-signing a short-lived JWT client assertion with the private key at private_key_path (0600, NEVER on argv or an env-var value). refresh_token_env is null by design here, not an oversight. Generate the key/cert pair in YOUR shell (never this process): openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -sha256 -nodes -days 730 -keyout netsuite-<entity>.key -out netsuite-<entity>.cert.pem -- then upload the .cert.pem in NetSuite (Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations > OAuth 2.0 > Certificate) and record the certificate id NetSuite assigns as cert_id above.
auth_netsuite_m2m_variant._suiteql_hints: TB query endpoint: POST /services/rest/query/v1/suiteql. Header 'Prefer: transient' is REQUIRED. Query text goes in the request body field q, not the URL. Page with limit/offset; hard cap 100000 results per query -- scope by period/subsidiary rather than paging past it. Balance-sheet account types need a CUMULATIVE-from-inception sum through the target period; income-statement account types need a PERIOD-scoped sum -- a naive period-only SUM silently understates every BS account while the TB still foots to zero. See scripts/connectors/suiteql.py build_tb_query.
auth_netsuite_m2m_variant._role_hints: Minimum NetSuite role: REST Web Services + Log in using Access Tokens + SuiteAnalytics Workbook + Lists -> Accounts (View) + the target subsidiary assignment. A role missing SuiteAnalytics Workbook is the most common cause of an otherwise-valid SuiteQL call returning an empty or denied result.
auth_netsuite_m2m_variant._settling_gate_note: Every numeric/date fact referenced in this block's notes is [unverified -- settling gate] per ../knowledge/finance-elt-connector-facts.md -- browser-verify against your own NetSuite sandbox before it gates a live build.

extract._note: Advisory rate/pagination hints — VERIFY against the connector-facts settling gates before relying on them.
extract.rate_limit_hint: per source — QBO ~500 req/min/realmId; Xero 60/min + 5 concurrent + 5000/day/tenant; NetSuite concurrency ~1 default; Intacct XML readByQuery <=2000 rows/call
extract.page_size_hint: NetSuite SuiteQL ~1000 rows/page; Intacct readByQuery pagination via resultId

close_period_watermark.note: The close period this extract belongs to. Stamped on every staged row (the canonical `period` column) so the staging table is partitionable / incrementally loadable by close period. Override at stage time with --period.

output._canonical_columns_note: Byte-identical to the dbt stg_trial_balance model. Do not reorder or rename.
